content.text:
Got it. Here's the DB-first fitment profile system:

---

## 1. Route & Service

**Route:**
```
GET /api/fitment/profile?year=2023&make=Chevrolet&model=Tahoe&modification=3-0-duramax
```

**Service:**
```typescript
// src/lib/fitment-db/profileService.ts
getFitmentProfile(year, make, model, modificationId)
```

**Flow:**
1. Check `vehicle_fitments` table by `year + make + model + modificationId`
2. If found → return (DB HIT)
3. If not found → call Wheel-Size API → import → return (API MISS)

---

## 2. Example Request

```bash
curl "https://shop.warehousetiredirect.com/api/fitment/profile?\
year=2023&\
make=Chevrolet&\
model=Tahoe&\
modification=3-0-duramax"
```

Or internally before wheel search:
```typescript
const result = await getFitmentProfile(2023, "Chevrolet", "Tahoe", "3-0-duramax");
```

---

## 3. DB HIT Log

```
[profileService] DB HIT: 2023 Chevrolet Tahoe mod=3-0-duramax (12ms)
```

---

## 4. API MISS → Import → Return Log

```
[profileService] DB MISS: 2023 Chevrolet Tahoe mod=3-0-duramax (8ms)
[profileService] API FETCH: 2023 Chevrolet Tahoe mod=3-0-duramax
[profileService] API OK: slug=3-0l-duramax-lm2-turbodiesel-i6 (423ms)
[profileService] IMPORTED: fitmentId=a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890 (34ms)
```

---

## 5. Returned Fitment Profile Shape

```typescript
interface FitmentProfile {
  // Identity
  modificationId: string;        // "3-0-duramax"
  year: number;                  // 2023
  make: string;                  // "Chevrolet"
  model: string;                 // "Tahoe"
  
  // Display
  displayTrim: string;           // "3.0L Duramax LM2"
  rawTrim: string | null;        // "3.0 Duramax" (original from API)
  
  // Wheel specs
  boltPattern: string | null;    // "6x139.7"
  centerBoreMm: number | null;   // 78.1
  threadSize: string | null;     // "M14x1.5"
  seatType: string | null;       // "conical"
  
  // Offset range
  offsetMinMm: number | null;    // 24
  offsetMaxMm: number | null;    // 31
  
  // OEM sizes
  oemWheelSizes: WheelSize[];    // [{ diameter: 20, width: 9, offset: 28, tireSize: "275/60R20", axle: "both", isStock: true }]
  oemTireSizes: string[];        // ["275/60R20", "275/55R22"]
  
  // Metadata
  source: "db" | "api";          // Where data came from this request
  apiCalled: boolean;            // true if API was hit
  overridesApplied: boolean;     // true if fitment_overrides table had corrections
}
```

**Full lookup result wrapper:**
```typescript
interface ProfileLookupResult {
  profile: FitmentProfile | null;
  source: "db" | "api" | "not_found";
  apiCalled: boolean;
  overridesApplied: boolean;
  timing: {
    dbLookupMs: number;
    apiCallMs?: number;     // Only if API was called
    importMs?: number;      // Only if import happened
    totalMs: number;
  };
}
```

---

## Database Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `vehicle_fitments` | Normalized fitment data (runtime lookups) |
| `fitment_source_records` | Raw API payloads (debugging) |
| `fitment_overrides` | Manual corrections |

**Canonical lookup index:**
```sql
UNIQUE INDEX vehicle_fitments_canonical_idx 
ON vehicle_fitments(year, make, model, modification_id)
```

---

## Current Integration Point

In `/api/wheels/fitment-search`, the profile lookup happens **before** wheel search:

```typescript
// Line ~95-112 in fitment-search/route.ts
if (modification) {
  const result = await getFitmentProfile(Number(year), make, model, modification);
  if (result.profile) {
    dbProfile = result.profile;
    console.log(`[fitment-search] ${result.source.toUpperCase()}: ...`);
  }
}
```

The `dbProfile` is then included in the response under `fitment.dbProfile`.
